    Functions:
    • variable
      • variable(index: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/Int///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), value: TypeParameter(dri=space.kscience.kmath.expressions/DSAlgebra///PointingToGenericParameters(0)/, name=T, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Build an instance representing a variable.Instances built using this constructor are considered to be the free variables with respect to which differentials are computed. As such, their differential with respect to themselves is +1.
    • ofDerivatives
      • ofDerivatives(derivatives: TypeParameter(dri=space.kscience.kmath.expressions/DSAlgebra///PointingToGenericParameters(0)/, name=T, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Build an instance from all its derivatives.
      • @param derivatives - derivatives sorted according to DSCompiler.getPartialDerivativeIndex.
    • const
      • const(value: TypeParameter(dri=space.kscience.kmath.expressions/DSAlgebra///PointingToGenericParameters(0)/, name=T, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • A constant expression that does not depend on arguments.
    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.
    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(symbol: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.expressions/Symbol///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
    • bindSymbol
      • bindSymbol(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • The same as bindSymbolOrNull but throws an error if symbol could not be bound
    • bindSymbol
      • bindSymbol(symbol: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.expressions/Symbol///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
    • derivative
      • derivative(symbols: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin.collections/List///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
    • derivative
      • derivative(symbols: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.expressions/Symbol///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
    • unaryOperationFunction
      • unaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperation: for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • unaryOperation
      • unaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.expressions/DS///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[, ], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperationFunction: i.e., for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param arg - the argument of operation.
      • the result of the operation.
    • binaryOperationFunction
      • binaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperation: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • binaryOperation
      • binaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), left: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.expressions/DS///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[, ], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), right: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.expressions/DS///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[, ], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperationFunction: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param left - the first argument of operation.
      • @param right - the second argument of operation.
      • a result of operation.
    • export
      • export(arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.expressions/DS///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[, ], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.

      • Build an instance representing a variable.Instances built using this constructor are considered to be the free variables with respect to which differentials are computed. As such, their differential with respect to themselves is +1.

      • @param operation - the name of operation.

      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.

    • getPartialDerivativeIndex
      • getPartialDerivativeIndex(orders: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/Int///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Get the index of a partial derivative in the array.If all orders are set to 0, then the 0<sup>th</sup> order derivative is returned, which is the value of the function.The indices of derivatives are between 0 and getSize − 1. Their specific order is fixed for a given compiler, but otherwise not publicly specified. There are however some simple cases which have guaranteed indices:the index of 0<sup>th</sup> order derivative is always 0if there is only 1 freeParameters, then the derivatives are sorted in increasing derivation order (i.e., f at index 0, df/dp at index 1, d<sup>2</sup>f/dp<sup>2</sup> at index 2 … d<sup>k</sup>f/dp<sup>k</sup> at index k),if the order is 1, then the derivatives are sorted in increasing free parameter order (i.e., f at index 0, df/dx<sub>1</sub> at index 1, df/dx<sub>2</sub> at index 2 … df/dx<sub>k</sub> at index k),all other cases are not publicly specified.This method is the inverse of method getPartialDerivativeOrders.
      • @param orders - derivation orders with respect to each parameter.
      • index of the partial derivative.
    • getPartialDerivativeOrders
      • getPartialDerivativeOrders(index: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/Int///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Get the derivation orders for a specific index in the array.This method is the inverse of getPartialDerivativeIndex.
      • @param index - of the partial derivative
      • orders derivation orders with respect to each parameter

    • const
      • const(value: TypeParameter(dri=space.kscience.kmath.expressions/ExpressionAlgebra///PointingToGenericParameters(0)/, name=T, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • A constant expression that does not depend on arguments.
    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.
    • bindSymbol
      • bindSymbol(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • The same as bindSymbolOrNull but throws an error if symbol could not be bound
    • unaryOperationFunction
      • unaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperation: for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • unaryOperation
      • unaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), arg: TypeParameter(dri=space.kscience.kmath.expressions/ExpressionAlgebra///PointingToGenericParameters(1)/, name=E, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperationFunction: i.e., for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param arg - the argument of operation.
      • the result of the operation.
    • binaryOperationFunction
      • binaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperation: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • binaryOperation
      • binaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), left: TypeParameter(dri=space.kscience.kmath.expressions/ExpressionAlgebra///PointingToGenericParameters(1)/, name=E, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), right: TypeParameter(dri=space.kscience.kmath.expressions/ExpressionAlgebra///PointingToGenericParameters(1)/, name=E, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperationFunction: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param left - the first argument of operation.
      • @param right - the second argument of operation.
      • a result of operation.
    • export
      • export(arg: TypeParameter(dri=space.kscience.kmath.expressions/ExpressionAlgebra///PointingToGenericParameters(1)/, name=E, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.

      • export(arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.expressions/Expression///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.

      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.

      • Dynamically dispatches a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperation: for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).

      • export(arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.expressions/MST///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.

      • unaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.expressions/MST///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperationFunction: i.e., for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param arg - the argument of operation.
      • the result of the operation.

      • Dynamically invokes a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperationFunction: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.

      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.

      • binaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperation: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.

      • @param operation - the name of operation.

      • export(arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.expressions/MST///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.

    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.

    • derive
      • derive(value: TypeParameter(dri=space.kscience.kmath.expressions/SimpleAutoDiffField/derive/#TypeParam(bounds=[kotlin.Any?])#kotlin.Function2[TypeParam(bounds=[space.kscience.kmath.operations.Field[TypeParam(bounds=[kotlin.Any])]]),TypeParam(bounds=[kotlin.Any?]),kotlin.Unit]/PointingToGenericParameters(0)/, name=R, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), block: FunctionalTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/Function2///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[, , ], isExtensionFunction=true, isSuspendable=false, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={}), contextParametersCount=0))
      • Performs update of derivative after the rest of the formula in the back-pass.For example, implementation of sin function is:fun AD.sin(x: Variable): Variable = derive(Variable(sin(x.x)) { z -> // call derive with function result x.d += z.d * cos(x.x) // update derivative using chain rule and derivative of the function}

    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.
    • bindSymbol
      • bindSymbol(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))

    • export
      • export(arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.expressions/AutoDiffValue///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.

    • const
      • const(value: TypeParameter(dri=space.kscience.kmath.expressions/SimpleAutoDiffExtendedField///PointingToGenericParameters(0)/, name=T, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))

    • derive
      • derive(value: TypeParameter(dri=space.kscience.kmath.expressions/SimpleAutoDiffExtendedField/derive/#TypeParam(bounds=[kotlin.Any?])#kotlin.Function2[TypeParam(bounds=[space.kscience.kmath.operations.ExtendedField[TypeParam(bounds=[kotlin.Any])]]),TypeParam(bounds=[kotlin.Any?]),kotlin.Unit]/PointingToGenericParameters(0)/, name=R, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), block: FunctionalTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/Function2///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[, , ], isExtensionFunction=true, isSuspendable=false, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={}), contextParametersCount=0))
      • Performs update of derivative after the rest of the formula in the back-pass.For example, implementation of sin function is:fun AD.sin(x: Variable): Variable = derive(Variable(sin(x.x)) { z -> // call derive with function result x.d += z.d * cos(x.x) // update derivative using chain rule and derivative of the function}

      • If the structure holds given attribute return itself. Otherwise, return a new Matrix that contains a computed attribute.This method is used to compute and cache attribute inside the structure. If one needs an attribute only once, better use Structure2D.getOrComputeAttribute.

    • attributeFor
      • attributeFor(structure: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[org.jetbrains.dokka.model.Star@42b3d4a9], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), attribute: TypeParameter(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/AlgebraND/attributeFor/#space.kscience.kmath.nd.StructureND[*]#TypeParam(bounds=[space.kscience.kmath.nd.StructureAttribute[TypeParam(bounds=[kotlin.Any?])]])/PointingToGenericParameters(1)/, name=A, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Get an attribute value for the structure in this scope. Structure features take precedence other context features.
      • @param structure - the structure.
      • @param attribute - to be computed.
      • a feature object or null if it isn't present.
    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.
    • bindSymbol
      • bindSymbol(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • The same as bindSymbolOrNull but throws an error if symbol could not be bound
    • unaryOperationFunction
      • unaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperation: for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • unaryOperation
      • unaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperationFunction: i.e., for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param arg - the argument of operation.
      • the result of the operation.
    • binaryOperationFunction
      • binaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperation: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • binaryOperation
      • binaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), left: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), right: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperationFunction: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param left - the first argument of operation.
      • @param right - the second argument of operation.
      • a result of operation.
    • export
      • export(arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.
    • add

    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.
    • bindSymbol
      • bindSymbol(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • The same as bindSymbolOrNull but throws an error if symbol could not be bound
    • unaryOperation
      • unaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperationFunction: i.e., for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param arg - the argument of operation.
      • the result of the operation.
    • binaryOperation
      • binaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), left: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), right: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperationFunction: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param left - the first argument of operation.
      • @param right - the second argument of operation.
      • a result of operation.
    • export
      • export(arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.
    • mutableStructureND
      • mutableStructureND(shape: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/ShapeND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), initializer: FunctionalTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/Function2///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[, , ], isExtensionFunction=true, isSuspendable=false, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={}), contextParametersCount=0))

    • unaryOperationFunction
      • unaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperation: for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • binaryOperationFunction
      • binaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperation: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.
    • bindSymbol
      • bindSymbol(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • The same as bindSymbolOrNull but throws an error if symbol could not be bound
    • unaryOperation
      • unaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperationFunction: i.e., for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param arg - the argument of operation.
      • the result of the operation.
    • binaryOperation
      • binaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), left: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), right: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperationFunction: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param left - the first argument of operation.
      • @param right - the second argument of operation.
      • a result of operation.
    • export
      • export(arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.

    • unaryOperationFunction
      • unaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperation: for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • binaryOperationFunction
      • binaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperation: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.
    • bindSymbol
      • bindSymbol(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • The same as bindSymbolOrNull but throws an error if symbol could not be bound
    • unaryOperation
      • unaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperationFunction: i.e., for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param arg - the argument of operation.
      • the result of the operation.
    • binaryOperation
      • binaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), left: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), right: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperationFunction: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param left - the first argument of operation.
      • @param right - the second argument of operation.
      • a result of operation.
    • export
      • export(arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.

      • bindSymbol(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • The same as bindSymbolOrNull but throws an error if symbol could not be bound
    • unaryOperation
      • unaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperationFunction: i.e., for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param arg - the argument of operation.
      • the result of the operation.
    • binaryOperation
      • binaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), left: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), right: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperationFunction: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param left - the first argument of operation.
      • @param right - the second argument of operation.
      • a result of operation.
    • export
      • export(arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.

      • Raises arg to a power if possible (negative number could not be raised to a fractional power). Throws IllegalArgumentException if not possible.
    • unaryOperationFunction
      • unaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperation: for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • binaryOperationFunction
      • binaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperation: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.
    • bindSymbol
      • bindSymbol(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • The same as bindSymbolOrNull but throws an error if symbol could not be bound
    • unaryOperation
      • unaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperationFunction: i.e., for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param arg - the argument of operation.
      • the result of the operation.
    • binaryOperation
      • binaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), left: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), right: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperationFunction: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param left - the first argument of operation.
      • @param right - the second argument of operation.
      • a result of operation.
    • export
      • export(arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.

    • unaryOperationFunction
      • unaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperation: for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • binaryOperationFunction
      • binaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperation: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.
    • bindSymbol
      • bindSymbol(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • The same as bindSymbolOrNull but throws an error if symbol could not be bound
    • unaryOperation
      • unaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperationFunction: i.e., for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param arg - the argument of operation.
      • the result of the operation.

      • binaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), left: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), right: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperationFunction: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param left - the first argument of operation.
      • @param right - the second argument of operation.
      • a result of operation.
    • export
      • export(arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.

    • unaryOperationFunction
      • unaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperation: for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • binaryOperationFunction
      • binaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperation: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.
    • bindSymbol
      • bindSymbol(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • The same as bindSymbolOrNull but throws an error if symbol could not be bound
    • unaryOperation
      • unaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperationFunction: i.e., for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param arg - the argument of operation.
      • the result of the operation.
    • binaryOperation
      • binaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), left: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), right: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.nd/StructureND///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperationFunction: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param left - the first argument of operation.
      • @param right - the second argument of operation.
      • a result of operation.

    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.

    Functions:
    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.
    • bindSymbol
      • bindSymbol(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • The same as bindSymbolOrNull but throws an error if symbol could not be bound
    • unaryOperationFunction
      • unaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperation: for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • unaryOperation
      • unaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), arg: TypeParameter(dri=space.kscience.kmath.operations/Algebra///PointingToGenericParameters(0)/, name=T, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperationFunction: i.e., for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param arg - the argument of operation.
      • the result of the operation.
    • binaryOperationFunction
      • binaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperation: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • binaryOperation
      • binaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), left: TypeParameter(dri=space.kscience.kmath.operations/Algebra///PointingToGenericParameters(0)/, name=T, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), right: TypeParameter(dri=space.kscience.kmath.operations/Algebra///PointingToGenericParameters(0)/, name=T, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperationFunction: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param left - the first argument of operation.
      • @param right - the second argument of operation.
      • a result of operation.
    • export
      • export(arg: TypeParameter(dri=space.kscience.kmath.operations/Algebra///PointingToGenericParameters(0)/, name=T, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.
    • add
      • add(left: TypeParameter(dri=space.kscience.kmath.operations/GroupOps///PointingToGenericParameters(0)/, name=T, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), right: TypeParameter(dri=space.kscience.kmath.operations/GroupOps///PointingToGenericParameters(0)/, name=T, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Addition of two elements.
      • @param left - the augend.
      • @param right - the addend.
      • the sum.

    • unaryOperationFunction
      • unaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperation: for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.
    • bindSymbol
      • bindSymbol(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • The same as bindSymbolOrNull but throws an error if symbol could not be bound
    • unaryOperation
      • unaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), arg: TypeParameter(dri=space.kscience.kmath.operations/RingOps///PointingToGenericParameters(0)/, name=T, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperationFunction: i.e., for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param arg - the argument of operation.
      • the result of the operation.

      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param left - the first argument of operation.
      • @param right - the second argument of operation.

      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.

      • unaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperation: for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.

    • binaryOperationFunction
      • binaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperation: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.
    • bindSymbol
      • bindSymbol(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • The same as bindSymbolOrNull but throws an error if symbol could not be bound

      • Dynamically invokes a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperationFunction: i.e., for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).

    • export
      • export(arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.operations/BigInt///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.

    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.
    • bindSymbol
      • bindSymbol(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • The same as bindSymbolOrNull but throws an error if symbol could not be bound
    • unaryOperation
      • unaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.structures/Buffer///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperationFunction: i.e., for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param arg - the argument of operation.
      • the result of the operation.
    • binaryOperation
      • binaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), left: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.structures/Buffer///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), right: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.structures/Buffer///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperationFunction: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param left - the first argument of operation.
      • @param right - the second argument of operation.
      • a result of operation.
    • export
      • export(arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.structures/Buffer///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.

    • unaryOperationFunction
      • unaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperation: for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • rightSideNumberOperationFunction
      • rightSideNumberOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a binary operation with the certain name with numeric first argument.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to rightSideNumberOperation: for any a, b, and c, rightSideNumberOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == leftSideNumberOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.

    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.
    • bindSymbol
      • bindSymbol(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • The same as bindSymbolOrNull but throws an error if symbol could not be bound
    • unaryOperation
      • unaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.structures/Buffer///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperationFunction: i.e., for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param arg - the argument of operation.
      • the result of the operation.
    • binaryOperation
      • binaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), left: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.structures/Buffer///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), right: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.structures/Buffer///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperationFunction: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param left - the first argument of operation.
      • @param right - the second argument of operation.
      • a result of operation.
    • export
      • export(arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.structures/Buffer///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.

      • binaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), left: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.structures/Buffer///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), right: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.structures/Buffer///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperationFunction: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).

      • @param left - the first argument of operation.
      • @param right - the second argument of operation.
      • a result of operation.

      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.

    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.
    • unaryOperation
      • unaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), arg: TypeParameter(dri=space.kscience.kmath.operations/LogicAlgebra///PointingToGenericParameters(0)/, name=T, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperationFunction: i.e., for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param arg - the argument of operation.
      • the result of the operation.

      • unaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperation: for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • binaryOperationFunction
      • binaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperation: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.
