      • unaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperation: for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • binaryOperationFunction
      • binaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperation: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.

    • export
      • export(arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.structures/Int16///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.

    • unaryOperationFunction
      • unaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperation: for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • binaryOperationFunction
      • binaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperation: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.
    • bindSymbol
      • bindSymbol(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • The same as bindSymbolOrNull but throws an error if symbol could not be bound
    • unaryOperation
      • unaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.structures/Int64///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperationFunction: i.e., for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param arg - the argument of operation.
      • the result of the operation.
    • binaryOperation
      • binaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), left: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.structures/Int64///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), right: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.structures/Int64///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperationFunction: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param left - the first argument of operation.
      • @param right - the second argument of operation.
      • a result of operation.
    • export
      • export(arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=space.kscience.kmath.structures/Int64///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.

    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.
    • bindSymbol
      • bindSymbol(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • The same as bindSymbolOrNull but throws an error if symbol could not be bound
    • unaryOperation
      • unaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), arg: TypeParameter(dri=space.kscience.kmath.operations/ExtendedField///PointingToGenericParameters(0)/, name=T, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperationFunction: i.e., for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param arg - the argument of operation.
      • the result of the operation.
    • binaryOperation
      • binaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), left: TypeParameter(dri=space.kscience.kmath.operations/ExtendedField///PointingToGenericParameters(0)/, name=T, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), right: TypeParameter(dri=space.kscience.kmath.operations/ExtendedField///PointingToGenericParameters(0)/, name=T, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperationFunction: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param left - the first argument of operation.
      • @param right - the second argument of operation.
      • a result of operation.
    • export
      • export(arg: TypeParameter(dri=space.kscience.kmath.operations/ExtendedField///PointingToGenericParameters(0)/, name=T, presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.

    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.

      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.

      • Addition of two elements.
      • the augend.
      • @param arg - the addend.
      • the sum.
    • minus
      • minus(arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/Short///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Subtraction of two elements.
      • the minuend.
      • @param arg - the subtrahend.
      • the difference.
    • times
      • times(arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/Short///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Multiplies this element by scalar.
      • the multiplier.
      • @param arg - the multiplicand.
    • power
      • power(arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/Short///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), pow: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/UInt///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Raises arg to the integer power pow.
    • unaryPlus
      • unaryPlus()
      • Returns this value.
      • this value.
      • this value.
    • unaryOperationFunction
      • unaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperation: for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • binaryOperationFunction
      • binaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperation: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.
    • bindSymbol
      • bindSymbol(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • The same as bindSymbolOrNull but throws an error if symbol could not be bound
    • unaryOperation
      • unaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/Short///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperationFunction: i.e., for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param arg - the argument of operation.
      • the result of the operation.
    • binaryOperation
      • binaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), left: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/Short///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), right: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/Short///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperationFunction: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param left - the first argument of operation.
      • @param right - the second argument of operation.
      • a result of operation.
    • export
      • export(arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/Short///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.
    • leftSideNumberOperationFunction
      • leftSideNumberOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a binary operation with the certain name with numeric first argument.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to leftSideNumberOperation: for any a, b, and c, leftSideNumberOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == leftSideNumberOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • leftSideNumberOperation
      • leftSideNumberOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), left: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/Number///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), right: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/Short///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a binary operation with the certain name with numeric first argument.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to leftSideNumberOperation: for any a, b, and c, leftSideNumberOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == leftSideNumberOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param left - the first argument of operation.
      • @param right - the second argument of operation.
      • a result of operation.
    • rightSideNumberOperationFunction
      • rightSideNumberOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a binary operation with the certain name with numeric first argument.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to rightSideNumberOperation: for any a, b, and c, rightSideNumberOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == leftSideNumberOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • rightSideNumberOperation

      • @param operation - the name of operation.

      • plus(arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=java.math/BigInteger///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Addition of two elements.
      • the augend.
      • @param arg - the addend.
      • the sum.
    • unaryOperationFunction
      • unaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperation: for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • binaryOperationFunction
      • binaryOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperation: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • an operation.
    • bindSymbolOrNull
      • bindSymbolOrNull(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Wraps a raw string to T object. This method is designed for three purposes:Mathematical constants (e, pi).Variables for expression-like contexts (a, b, c…).Literals ({1, 2}, ((3; 4))).If algebra can't parse the string, then this method must throw kotlin.IllegalStateException.
      • @param value - the raw string.
      • an object or null if symbol could not be bound to the context.
    • bindSymbol
      • bindSymbol(value: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • The same as bindSymbolOrNull but throws an error if symbol could not be bound
    • unaryOperation
      • unaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=java.math/BigInteger///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperationFunction: i.e., for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param arg - the argument of operation.
      • the result of the operation.
    • binaryOperation
      • binaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), left: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=java.math/BigInteger///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), right: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=java.math/BigInteger///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperationFunction: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param left - the first argument of operation.
      • @param right - the second argument of operation.
      • a result of operation.
    • export
      • export(arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=java.math/BigInteger///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.

      • @param operation - the name of operation.

      • unaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=java.math/BigDecimal///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a unary operation with the certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to unaryOperationFunction: i.e., for any a and b, unaryOperationFunction(a)(b) == unaryOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param arg - the argument of operation.
      • the result of the operation.
    • binaryOperation
      • binaryOperation(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), left: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=java.math/BigDecimal///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})), right: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=java.math/BigDecimal///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically invokes a binary operation with a certain name.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then the function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to binaryOperationFunction: for any a, b, and c, binaryOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == binaryOperation(a, b, c).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
      • @param left - the first argument of operation.
      • @param right - the second argument of operation.
      • a result of operation.
    • export
      • export(arg: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=java.math/BigDecimal///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Export an algebra element, so it could be accessed even after algebra scope is closed. This method must be used on algebras where data is stored externally or any local algebra state is used. By default (if not overridden), exports the object itself.

      • leftSideNumberOperationFunction(operation: GenericTypeConstructor(dri=kotlin/String///PointingToDeclaration/, projections=[], presentableName=null, extra=PropertyContainer(map={})))
      • Dynamically dispatches a binary operation with the certain name with numeric first argument.Implementations must fulfil the following requirements:If operation is not defined in the structure, then function throws kotlin.IllegalStateException.Equivalence to leftSideNumberOperation: for any a, b, and c, leftSideNumberOperationFunction(a)(b, c) == leftSideNumberOperation(a, b).
      • @param operation - the name of operation.
